title: We Won't Face It Alone
tagline: The full moon rises. The Titan stirs. Everyone takes their position. And it turns out: they were never going to face it alone.
arc_note: SEASON 1 FINALE — All arcs resolve. Zeus seals east crack with precision thunderbolts (EP017 payoff). Hercules reinforces pressure points from engineering scrolls (EP016 payoff). Perseus guides Medusa to the surface cracks (EP014 payoff). Achilles coordinates from the adjusted position — heel aching, not breaking (EP019 payoff). Poseidon redirects the Titan toward the sea with the sea-draw plus his 'surprise.' Medusa stone-seals the surface cracks permanently. The Titan isn't evil — it just wants sky. The final scroll entry is confirmed: WE WON'T FACE IT ALONE. All running jokes pay off in the finale beat. Archie looks at the camera one final time.

scenes.title: The Moon Rises
scenes.narration: The full moon rose exactly when Athena had calculated. The ground shook once — deep, slow, enormous. The cups on the shelves rattled. The Olympus Sea moved in a wave that went the wrong direction. Athena opened the scroll to the final section. WHAT LIVES BELOW. Seven pages of preparation. At the bottom of the seventh page, the final entry, written three days ago: WE WON'T FACE IT ALONE. She closed the scroll. She gave the signal. Everyone took their position.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. SEASON FINALE OPENING — The full moon rising over Olympus. One deep ground shake — cups rattling, the Olympus Sea rolling wrong-direction. Athena at the scroll, opening to WHAT LIVES BELOW, seven pages visible. She reads the final entry. She closes the scroll with both hands. She gives the signal — one nod. The group disperses to positions. Epic, warm, slightly scary but not overwhelming. 16:9.

scenes.title: Everyone in Place
scenes.narration: Zeus took the east crack position. Forty meters. His thunderbolt aim had been practiced for two weeks precisely for this shot. Hercules went to the pressure points — four of them, all marked in his scroll, all reinforced exactly as the lever mechanics section described. Achilles set up coordination at the adjusted position, ten meters left of the original mark. His heel ached. He stood. Perseus found Medusa at the east valley edge — exactly where she had said she would be. She was there.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. POSITIONS MONTAGE — Zeus at east crack position measuring forty meters, thunderbolt ready. Hercules at first pressure point, scroll open, working with focused calm. Achilles at adjusted coordination position, heel aching but standing, coordination tablet in hand. Perseus arriving at east valley edge — and Medusa is already there. She came. She is exactly where she said she would be. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Lives Below
scenes.narration: The ground cracked at the east face. Not violently — slowly, enormously, the way mountains move when they decide to. What emerged was not a monster. It was ancient and vast and had been underground for three thousand years. It had no malice. What it had was an expression that Athena had never seen before — the same one her scroll could not describe — which turned out to be, unmistakably, wonder. It had never seen the sky. It was looking at the full moon. It had no idea anyone else was there.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. THE TITAN EMERGES — enormous, ancient, stone and deep-earth colors, but not monstrous. Slow. Deliberate. Its face, if it has a face, is turned toward the sky. Toward the full moon. It has not seen sky in three thousand years. Its expression is pure wonder. It has not noticed the group at all. The scale: it makes Olympus look small. But it is not threatening. It is just... incredibly, impossibly old. 16:9.

scenes.title: Everyone Does Their Part
scenes.narration: Achilles called the sequence. His heel was at its worst — he called it clearly anyway. Zeus fired three precision bolts at the east crack: center, center, center. The crack stopped spreading. Hercules reinforced all four pressure points in six minutes — his scroll propped open against a rock, notes checked once. The Titan began moving toward the mountain. Then: the Olympus Sea lit up. Poseidon had arrived. He had brought the sea-draw. He had also brought his surprise.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. SEQUENCE EXECUTING — Achilles calling coordination, heel visible as he stands firm. Zeus firing three precision bolts at the east crack — center, center, center, each one exactly right. Hercules at pressure points, scroll open, working systematically. The Titan moving toward the mountain. Then: the Olympus Sea blazing with bioluminescent light as Poseidon arrives below. The sea-draw beginning to pull. And something else: a second light source. The surprise. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Dolphins
scenes.narration: Poseidon's surprise was not additional sea magic. It was not a second wave. It was every dolphin that had ever visited the Olympus Sea, plus approximately forty new dolphins who had heard about it from the original dolphins and very much wanted to be involved. They lit the water. They made the sound — the specific frequency Poseidon had told them to make — that drew the Titan's attention from the mountain to the sea. The Titan looked down. It had also never seen dolphins. It was profoundly interested in the dolphins.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. POSEIDON'S SURPRISE — the full Olympus Sea erupting with dolphins: the original six, plus forty more, all lit up and making their directed sound in coordinated formation. The Titan turning from the mountain to look at the sea. Its expression, impossibly ancient, turning curious and gentle at the sight of the dolphins. It has never seen dolphins. The dolphins are very motivated. Poseidon in the sea below, trident raised, enormously proud of himself. 16:9.

scenes.title: She Keeps Her Word
scenes.narration: With the Titan drawn toward the sea and away from the mountain, Perseus guided Medusa along the crack line — telling her exactly where to stand, what to seal, when. She moved without hesitation. She sealed each surface crack permanently, stone-to-stone, precise and complete. She did not speak during it. When the last crack was sealed, she stood back. Perseus said: 'You did it.' She said: 'I said I would.' Then she said: 'Come visit when this is over. The west garden section. Perseus hasn't seen it yet.' He said: 'I will.' She left.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. MEDUSA AT WORK — Perseus guiding her along the crack line, she moving with complete precision and efficiency, sealing each crack with her stone ability. No drama, no hesitation. The last crack closed. Perseus's quiet: 'You did it.' Her: 'I said I would.' Then the invitation — the west garden, the thing she told Hercules about in EP014. Her leaving. Perseus watching her go. Her arc is not over. But this part of it is. 16:9.

scenes.title: WE WON'T FACE IT ALONE
scenes.narration: The Titan reached the Olympus Sea edge and looked at the sky reflected in the water for a very long time. Then it settled, vast and ancient, into the deep water — not gone, but resting, watching the sky through the water. The cracks were sealed. The pressure points held. Olympus was intact. Athena opened the scroll one more time. She read the final entry: WE WON'T FACE IT ALONE. She made one check mark. Archie looked at the camera. Zeus's crown was on straight for exactly four seconds before listing slightly left. It was fine. Everything was fine. Achilles's heel still hurt. He said he was fine. He said it differently than he used to. See you next season on Little Olympus.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. SEASON FINALE CLOSING — The Titan vast and settled in the deep Olympus Sea, watching sky-reflection, peaceful. The group together on the Olympus balcony: Zeus with the crown listing slightly left, Athena with the scroll and her check mark, Hercules already talking about what he read in the engineering notes, Perseus looking at the sea with something on his mind, Achilles standing easily (heel still aching, but easy now). Archie on Athena's shoulder looks at the camera: one long, satisfied slow blink. The Olympus Sea sparkles. Full moon. Season one: done. 16:9.

lesson: The people who show up when it matters most aren't always the ones you expected. Sometimes it's an uncle with dolphins. Sometimes it's someone you thought was an enemy. Sometimes it's yourself, standing on a hurt heel, calling the sequence anyway.

running_joke_payoffs.achilles_heel: Heel at its worst during the finale — he coordinates anyway. 'I'm fine' said differently at the end: not defensive, not dismissive. True.
running_joke_payoffs.archie_camera: Final camera look: one long slow blink. Satisfied. He knew they were ready.
running_joke_payoffs.athena_chin_taps: Three chin taps when she sees all cracks sealed — first time she's done it and smiled.
running_joke_payoffs.hercules_i_helped: After the pressure points: he says 'I helped!' but adds '— I know I helped.' He does know. It's different.
running_joke_payoffs.zeus_crown: Crown on straight for exactly four seconds before listing left. Right after the finale. Perfect.

arc_completions.zeus: Precision thunderbolts used exactly as trained. Three shots, three centers. He was useful. He prepared for something he couldn't name and it was exactly the right thing.
arc_completions.achilles: Told the truth about his heel. The position was moved. He stood on it anyway. Arc: learning that strength includes honesty, not just performance.
arc_completions.hercules: Engineering scrolls used under real pressure. His notes, his marks, his sequence. Not absorbed from over someone's shoulder — his own. He earned this one.
arc_completions.perseus: Medusa came. She sealed the cracks. He guided her. She invited him to the west garden. Their relationship: real, continued, unresolved in the best way.
arc_completions.medusa: She kept her word. She stayed exactly who she is — not reformed, not villain, just a person who made a choice when it mattered. Arc: open.
arc_completions.athena: Her plan worked because it used what everyone actually had. WE WON'T FACE IT ALONE: confirmed.

season_2_setup: The Titan is in the Olympus Sea, watching the sky through the water. It is not contained. It is resting. Poseidon knows it's there. The dolphins visit it. Zeus's father knows something is coming — 'It's not time yet, but it will be.' Season 2: something in the deep sea stirs. And Medusa's west garden has something Perseus hasn't seen yet.
youtube_title: THE SEASON FINALE — We Won't Face It Alone 🌕⚡ | Little Olympus Episode 20
youtube_description:
This is it. The full moon rises. The Titan stirs.

Zeus has practiced for two weeks for exactly this moment.
Hercules studied engineering from upside-down scrolls.
Achilles told the truth about his heel.
Perseus asked Medusa, and she said yes.
And Poseidon brought a surprise.

SEASON 1 FINALE — every episode paid off today.

See you in Season 2. 🐬
